compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, I can convert a non-epic ticket to a full epic via brainstorm
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the convert-to-epic path in brainstorm — close the original ticket as superseded and invoke the normal brainstorm epic flow to create a new epic.
**Why**: Practitioners with a story/task/bug that turns out to need full epic treatment can seamlessly promote it without losing context.
**Scope**:
- IN: Close original ticket as superseded (with traceability reference to new epic), invoke full brainstorm epic flow (Socratic dialogue, approach selection, scrutiny pipeline, fidelity review, ticket creation), handle bug-specific close requirements, handle tickets with open children
- OUT: Enrich-in-place path (Story 3), type detection (Story 1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, selecting "convert to epic" closes the original ticket as superseded and creates a new epic via the normal brainstorm flow
  ← Satisfies: "Convert-to-epic path: closes the original ticket as superseded, creates a new epic via the normal brainstorm flow"
- When this story is complete, the new epic's description references the original ticket ID for traceability
  ← Satisfies: "Convert-to-epic path: closes the original ticket as superseded"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] If epic creation fails mid-way, the original ticket is already closed — consider closing the original only after the new epic is successfully created
- [Reliability] Bug ticket close requires --reason flag with prefix "Fixed:" or "Escalated to user:" — the convert flow must use an appropriate reason string (e.g., --reason="Escalated to user: superseded by epic <new-epic-id>")
- [Reliability] ticket-transition.sh blocks closing tickets with open children — if the original non-epic ticket has child tasks, the convert flow must re-parent them to the new epic or close them before closing the original
